TextENGNew: As I sat on the tatami in a daze, composure gradually returned to me.
My shirt clung to my body, drenched in sweat and humidity.
What had just happened... what was any of that...?
Slowly, my mind began to turn again.
Sonozaki-san sat a short distance away, tending to the sword she had just used.
Miyuki-chan was sitting next to Towada-san as though nothing had happened.
Otobe, still not recovered from the adrenaline of the life-and-death encounter moments ago, was breathing hard, seemingly waiting for someone to speak first.
Eventually... it was Sonozaki-san who broke the silence.
``Oh, right.@k@vS10/43/410400446. ...About my car.``
changeReason: Old adds 'As you know, I went looking for my car' not in the Japanese.

TextENGNew: "My car is gone."
...As soon as we heard that report, we all fell silent.
Hey, come on... isn't that an awfully terrible thing to just come out and say?!
"What do you mean...?"
"I mean it disappeared.@k@vS10/43/410400449. I'm certain that I parked it somewhere not too far from the base of the steps leading to this shrine."
"Then... what does that mean?!"
"That's what I'm trying to say.@k@vS10/43/410400451. ...Maybe my car was demoned away?@k@vS10/43/410400452. Hehehe..."
"N-no way..."
Sonozaki-san's laughter echoed through the quiet assembly hall.
I didn't have a clue what Sonozaki-san was laughing about.
Had she simply surrendered to despair? Or maybe she actually found enjoyment in this situation, being isolated from the outside world with our lives on the line...?
"Could you give me a proper answer?!@k@vS10/62/410101324. What is it with you?!"

"You're hiding something from us, aren't you?@k@vS10/62/410101326. What about those guys from before...? What's the deal with them?!@k@vS10/62/410101327. You must've known something. That's why you lent me this gun, right?"
I shouted in frustration.
But Sonozaki-san remained silent and calmly listened to what I had to say.
Her composure... made me all the more irritated.
"Can you give me a straight answer?@k@vS10/62/410101329. I still have no idea who you are, but those guys were yakuza, weren't they?"
"Have we gotten ourselves involved in a yakuza conflict?"
Still frustrated, I tossed out the theory I came up with just a little while ago.
I wasn't sure how close I was to the truth, but I played my cards anyway... I was at my limit.
All of our cars had vanished. Our retreat was cut off, and I wasn't the sort of person who could survive a night of life or death battle.
"Arakawa... do you have any evidence for that accusation?"
"E-evidence...?!"
"...Anyway, Arakawa,@k@vS10/43/410400455. you look tired.@k@vS10/43/410400456. Why not get a little rest?"
There wasn't the slightest trace of kindness or gratitude in Sonozaki-san's tone.
What she meant by that was... "Shut up."
But that wasn't enough to convince me.
The fear and confusion had driven my patience beyond the limit, and even though I was terrified of Sonozaki-san until a little while ago, I burst out yelling at her.
"Our lives are in danger thanks to you!@k@vS10/62/410101333. Doesn't that warrant an explanation?@k@vS10/62/410101334. Right, Miyuki-chan?!"
"Huh? Yeah..."

"And that goes for you too, Miyuki-chan!@k@vS10/62/410101336. What on earth are you hiding from us?!@k@vS10/62/410101337. Are you actually a writer?!@k@vS10/62/410101338. Then how do you explain that gun?!"
Miyuki-chan awkwardly looked down and scratched her head, then finished counting the number of bullets left in the magazine of her handgun... and let out a light sigh.
...She told me she would "explain later," but it looked like she had no interest in explaining right now. In fact, she seemed far more interested in determining how many bullets she had left...?
"Wh-what is it, damn it!?@k@vS10/62/410101340. Stop hiding things and give me a proper answer!@k@vS10/62/410101341. Work with me here, people!"
"...Aren't you keeping secrets of your own?"
Sonozaki-san looked Otobe directly in the eyes as she said that. And when he noticed that... his shoulders slumped and he started to tremble.
"What do you mean...?"
"Everyone in this room is keeping secrets.@k@vS10/43/410400459. ...Right, Otobe?"
"...I-I'm not... hiding anything..."
Sonozaki-san looked at all of our faces, one by one, to make sure.
Miyuki-chan forced a smile as she slid the magazine into the handle of her gun.
Towada-san put a hand on her chest to still its beating.
What is this...? What's going on?
Was everyone here keeping secrets?!
"That goes for you too... right, Arakawa?"
"Huuh?! I... I'm not hiding anything, ok?"
"Hooh~?@k@vS10/43/410400462. ...Then let's hear it, Arakawa.@k@vS10/43/410400463. Why did you come to this village?"
"Why did I come here...?@k@vS10/62/410101346. I'm an occult writer. Didn't I tell you?@k@vS10/62/410101347. I'm doing investigative reporting."
"Well, that may be true.@k@vS10/43/410400465. Your secret is probably the most innocent among us."
"...But, why do you know so much... about the curse of this village?"
Right before my eyes, my hands started trembling restlessly. They struggled to maintain their grip on the plastic bottle I was holding.
A cold sweat seeped out of my armpits as she said that.
What...?
What was the meaning of that...?
No way... could she know about that?!
How?!
"Wh... what are you saying?@k@vS10/62/410101350. I have no idea what you're talking about!@k@vS10/62/410101351. Stop throwing around false accusations!"
Sonozaki-san looked me straight in the eyes as I said that, like she was issuing me a challenge.
But... I couldn't endure it for more than a second, so I dodged it by taking a sip of the tea in hand.

"Then I'll say it.@k@vS10/43/410400468. The reason you're fixated on Hinamizawa...@k@vS10/43/410400469. is because your father... was cursed by someone from Hinamizawa... and it ultimately killed him, right?"
*thunk*
I immediately dropped the plastic bottle.
H... how does she know about that...?!

"Guess I hit the nail on the head...@k@vS10/43/410400471. What happened between your father, the Sonozaki family's daughter, and the young boy who survived?@k@vS10/43/410400472. I won't let you say you don't know, all right?"
"W-why do you know about that?!"
"I'm the one asking questions here.@k@vS10/43/410400474. Go on, give me an answer. ...Well?"
"...!@k@vS10/62/410101355. I-I don't... know anything about that!@k@vS10/62/410101356. I don't!"
Judging by the tone of Sonozaki-san's voice, I got the impression she enjoyed watching me get upset.
I looked back at Sonozaki-san with a challenging gaze... but unfortunately, I couldn't disguise my excessively labored breathing.
Sonozaki-san's eyes had the stillness of a corpse.
I'd never seen a person with eyes like that before. Honestly... it was uncanny.
Long ago, my father was a reporter for a weekly photo magazine.
The fierce competition between rival magazines required intensive investigation in order to survive.
And so... in 1983, my father interviewed a boy and a girl who were fortunate enough to survive the Hinamizawa gas disaster.
The boy was the sole survivor of the school bombing incident perpetrated by another female student.
Then, police brought him to the Okinomiya police station to hear his testimony about the incident. He spent the whole night there... which is why he survived the gas disaster the next day.
But he suffered intense mental trauma from losing his parents, classmates, and the entire village all at once... so the boy was hospitalized shortly thereafter.
And though the girl lost her sister in the bombing incident, she escaped harm thanks to returning to her home in Okinomiya... But, she was just as traumatized as the boy.
So, my father snuck into their hospitals and heavy-handedly coerced them into telling him their stories.
...But the boy died a few days after his interview. The cause of death was unknown, but I later heard he was killed by the "curse."
And not only that, but the content of those interviews included eerie and mysterious predictions, which ultimately gave more weight to the "curse" theory.
Shortly after that, my father suffered mental instability. He was dismissed from one case after another... and in the end, he couldn't even write a coherent sentence, so they fired him.
After that, he returned to his hometown and spent his days lazing about... Ultimately, he drowned to death just as the girl he interviewed prophesized.
Right... That's right, I remember...!
I'm certain that girl's last name was "Sonozaki"...
So, she must be a relative of this Mion Sonozaki...!
Many things happened after that, but in the end my grandparents gave me my father's diary and interview materials.
I took those bizarre and eerie materials and compiled them into a story about weird supernatural phenomena... then submitted it to the editorial department.
That article garnered a surprisingly strong reception. And it had a bit of a surge in popularity once it got posted on some news site online.
That story caused the Hinamizawa occult boom to catch fire, and finally earned me a name as an occult writer...
Even though my father was the one who worked so hard to gather that intel... in reality, I was the one to benefit from it.
In a sense, I received all the recognition that my father should have received, but that outcome couldn't be helped.
But, what happened to my father when he interviewed those people from Hinamizawa...?
Who were those survivors my father spoke to...?
Even if it wasn't part of my job, I absolutely wanted to know.
So, I had to figure out what my father was searching for... and that was why I decided to come to Hinamizawa today...
"Arakawa-san, what do you mean...?@k@vS10/41/410200585. Do you think... we're being punished for what your father did...?"
"Th-that's impossible!"
"Arakawa-san, what's wrong?@k@vS10/41/410200587. You look so pale..."
"I-it's nothing..."
My heart was pulsing like an alarm bell, and no matter how many times I sipped from my oolong tea, my throat quickly dried up.
I hadn't said anything.
So... how...? How did Sonozaki-san know about my father...?
"Arakawa-san... what on earth did your father do?@k@vS10/41/410200589. No way, did he do something terrible enough to deserve being cursed?!"
"...Hell no!"
I turned to Otobe and spat those words out.
He wanted to go throwing out accusations? Well so did I!!
"Now, let me ask... What about you?@k@vS10/62/410101361. You're hiding something too, aren't you?"
I pointed my spear toward Otobe and let loose.
Nervous, indecisive Otobe seemed like the easiest one to attack.
"I... haven't done anything to warrant being cursed."
"...Seriously...?"
"Yes, really..."
Otobe turned his gaze downward and cut off the conversation. But it was clear to all of us that he was putting on an act.
"Wow~."
"Then let me ask. What is this?"
Sonozaki-san thrust a single card right up to the tip of Otobe's nose. And it looked like it was a cash card.
Otobe gasped and his eyes opened wide.
Then he quickly checked his back pocket, and turned toward the cash card once more.
...Otobe's face was starting to develop a twitch.
"This fell out of your back pocket a little while ago."
"...I'll ask again.@k@vS10/43/410400480. Whose card is this?"
"Ah... t-that's...!"
"This name here... doesn't look like your name, does it?@k@vS10/43/410400482. ...It says Chiaki Hori.@k@vS10/43/410400483. So, who is this Chiaki?"
Otobe nervously fidgeted as he gazed at the card.
He reminded me of a small animal, standing face to face with its natural predator.
"So, explain yourself.@k@vS10/43/410400485. This thing fell out of your pocket, Otobe, so why does it have someone else's name written on it?"
"Hey, Otobe...?"
Otobe's face turned completely pale.
His heart was beating so hard it felt like I could hear it from the seat next to him.
Sonozaki-san continued staring Otobe directly in the eyes, and didn't look away for even a moment.
"Otobe...@k@vS10/43/410400487. What are you hiding?"
"N... nothing..."
"...Why don't you tell us the truth already?@k@vS10/40/410300768. Like when your cell phone broke...@k@vS10/40/410300769. You broke it yourself, didn't you, Otobe-kun?"
Bit by bit, Miyuki-chan continued her verbal assault. Otobe reacted sharply and looked up after each comment.

"All this time, I was wondering why only your phone was broken...@k@vS10/40/410300771. It's because it was the only one that could connect, wasn't it?@k@vS10/40/410300772. That sounds right... doesn't it?"
"Y... yeah, that's what I think."
"Then let me ask you this...@k@vS10/40/410300774. Otobe-kun... was your phone actually able to connect?"
"...?! I-It was able to connect, ok?@k@vS10/41/410200604. What are you even saying?!@k@vS10/41/410200605. I was talking to the police back then. You were listening, weren't you, Sorimachi-san?!"

"But isn't that a bit strange?@k@vS10/40/410300776. Otobe-kun, your phone is the same model as Sonozaki-san's, right?@k@vS10/40/410300777. If they're the same, why was your phone the only one that could connect?"
"I-I don't know...@k@vS10/41/410200607. That... might just be manufacturing variation?@k@vS10/41/410200608. I wouldn't know..."
"...Hmm, is that so?@k@vS10/40/410300779. But is that really what this is...?"
Otobe's lips were quivering.
Miyuki-chan continued putting pressure on Otobe.
...Her ruthless assault had even me shivering.
Otobe did his best to pretend he was still calm, but his attempt was woefully unsuccessful.
His labored breathing echoed through the entire assembly room... filling the surrounding area with a tense atmosphere.
"I think... it was because of manufacturing differences.@k@vS10/41/410200611. And..."
"And what...?"
"And... I'm certain I was able to connect earlier!@k@vS10/41/410200613. The antenna just happened to show a signal.@k@vS10/41/410200614. Even I don't know why it was able to connect...!"
Miyuki-chan took a deep breath.
My initial impression of Miyuki-chan as a lively, earnest, cute girl was all but gone.
Replaced with that... was the visage of a bird of prey, stalking a small, defenseless animal.
"...That... may be true."
"...Right?@k@vS10/41/410200616. So, there's nothing all that weird about it..."
Otobe's forced smile had a light twitch to it.
He must've misunderstood what Miyuki-chan was saying, and believed she was convinced.
...Or maybe he's trying to tell himself that?
However... Miyuki-chan wasn't about to let Otobe escape.
In fact... she was luring him to the final stage...
"...Oh, right.@k@vS10/40/410300784. Let me tell you one more thing."
"......This village is completely outside of the service area."
"It's an uninhabited village. There's no reason to have signal relays out here."

"Ah... and don't think you can get out of this by saying an old signal tower might have been working.@k@vS10/40/410300788. This whole area has been sealed off since 1983.@k@vS10/40/410300789. Back then, none of the villagers would've had cell phones."
"N-no way...@k@vS10/41/410200620. If you knew that... from the start... then why...?"
"I was waiting for you to come clean about it.@k@vS10/40/410300791. Honestly... I don't enjoy driving people into a corner like this.@k@vS10/40/410300792. But I couldn't keep quiet any longer."
"...Now, let me ask you once more.@k@vS10/40/410300794. Your phone... wasn't actually able to connect, was it?"
"...That's right..."
That was all Otobe could say in response.
Otobe dejectedly bowed his head. That gesture was an admission of defeat.

"...I'm sorry.@k@vS10/41/410200624. I... lied to you all.@k@vS10/41/410200625. ...It's just as Sorimachi-san says... My phone couldn't connect either.@k@vS10/41/410200626. ...I was just pretending to get through."
"Then, when you notified the police..."
"That was a lie...@k@vS10/41/410200628. I was just pretending to talk on the phone."
Otobe continued looking down as he said that, and thanks to that his face looked like a misbehaving child.
"I lied about contacting the police...@k@vS10/41/410200631. But if we make it out of this... we can do something... so..."
"Wait, hold on.@k@vS10/62/410101366. Does that mean you actually stole that cash card?"
"Sorry...@k@vS10/41/410200634. I didn't want the police involved... until after I got the money..."
"...That's why you broke your phone?"
"Right...@k@vS10/41/410200636. I was afraid someone might notice that my phone couldn't connect...@k@vS10/41/410200637. If that happened... I wouldn't be able to hide it any longer..."
"...That's not all you're hiding from us, is it?@k@vS10/40/410300797. Where did you get the cash card from?@k@vS10/40/410300798. ...No more secrets."
Otobe drooped even further down as Miyuki-chan asked that in a scathing tone.
He drooped more and more... until he couldn't go any further.
"Otobe. Tell us everything.@k@vS10/43/410400489. What are you so frightened about...?"
After a long pause... Otobe started saying "I'm sorry" over and over.
The sound echoed miserably here and there... with some sobbing mixed in.
"I'm sorry... I'm sorry... I'm sorry..."
"...So, what are you sorry about?"
"......I'm the worst...@k@vS10/41/410200643. ...I'm such a miserable excuse for a human being...!!"
Huge tears started accumulating in Otobe's eyes... and spilling to the ground.
Sonozaki-san gently stroked his trembling shoulder, then urged him on in a voice filled with tenderness, as deep as the ocean.
"Tell us... What happened?"
"...Okay.@k@vS10/41/410200645. ...It started in the spring of last year, when I came to Tokyo."
Otobe quietly began talking about his past.
...I never thought of myself as the kind of person who would stay in the countryside forever.
My father was a government worker and my mother was a housewife. Everyone in the area was acquainted with everyone else.
Amidst their strict values and well-defined human relationships, there was one value that was absolutely essential to survival. Cooperation.
I was worried I'd spend the rest of my life in such a tiny world, so I felt desperate.
I couldn't see anything in my future.
No dreams to aspire to.
My father tried to get me a job at a local company... but I hated it.
I figured I could probably work things out if I went to Tokyo...
So I poured my heart and soul into studying, then took an entrance exam for a university in Tokyo.

"Akira... country folk like you won't be able to get by in Tokyo.@k@vS10/00/VT3A_otobeM1002. Please, get a job around here, find a wife to settle down with, and make lots of cute grandchildren to make your father happy."
"...I don't think that way of living is right for me."
"Akira...?!"
"I've already decided.@k@vS10/41/410200648. If I get accepted to a school in Tokyo, I'm going.@k@vS10/41/410200649. But if I don't get in... then I'll stay here and do like you asked."
And then... I got accepted.
It was some university I had never heard of... but it was a college in Tokyo, so that was good enough for me.
"If you really did get accepted... then you have my support.@k@vS10/00/VT3A_otobeM1005. ...So, do your best."
Even though I expected my mother to be opposed... for some reason, she was pleased.
Although I intended to work to pay my tuition... my mother talked to my father about it and in the end they decided to pay for my tuition and living expenses.
"Study hard.@k@vS10/00/VT3A_otobeF1002. ...It's probably for the best to venture out of the local area once in your lifetime."
...To be honest... I couldn't believe my father would say something like that.
But they had a good reason for supporting me. ...I understood that.
My parents were convinced I'd return to the country in the future.
Traveling to Tokyo was just a momentary event. Just a little longer than a vacation. That way of thinking was what allowed them to send me off.

The money my parents gave me was like a chain shackled to my ankle. A debt to be repaid. They saw it as an investment, so that I'd be forced to return to the countryside right away if they were to ever take it away.
But I wouldn't stand for that.
I had no intention of ever going back there.
I intended to plant my roots in Tokyo once I got out of college.
"Akira, study hard.@k@vS10/00/VT3A_otobeM1007. Make a man of yourself quickly and make your father happy."
"Thank you for looking after me until now.@k@vS10/41/410200651. Take care, Mom."
"Akira, what are you talking about?!@k@vS10/00/VT3A_otobeM1009. Anyway, I'm sure life in Tokyo will be tough, so work hard out there."
"...You don't have to tell me to work hard.@k@vS10/41/410200653. See you later, Mom."
Mom came all the way to the local station to see me off, but I coldly sent her away.
...The countryside, my parents... I cast them all aside.
I took the first step toward my ambitions.
...Everything in Tokyo felt fresh.

Unlike high school, I didn't have to go to class early in the morning. They offered lectures in the afternoon and the evening. And it was easy to skip class and have a classmate answer the roll call in my place.
This city was nothing like how my parents complained about it.
Instead, it was full of fashionably dressed girls and classmates who felt far more worldly than me. And there were trendy shops and amusement districts.
Though I didn't have to pay for tuition, I did start working a part-time job to help cover my living expenses.
Every day, I'd wake up around noon, spend some time at the university, then work my part-time job until the evening.
My coworkers taught me all kinds of things. How to drink, how to dance at clubs, how to ask girls out...
After spending time with them, I started to develop an inferiority complex compared to my classmates. I felt like a child.
My coworkers introduced me to all kinds of girls.
Shortly after starting college, I began going out with a girl living in a luxury apartment complex in Aoyama.
She had a long slender neck, like a giraffe.
She dressed to match cutting-edge trends and wore lots of expensive accessories. She felt like a real Tokyo girl, and I liked that about her.
But she was an expensive girl.

She worked at a cabaret club so she brought in far more income than me, but she never opened her wallet for anything. Meals and entertainment were always on me. I believe that's a man's responsibility.
Shopping was one of her favorite pastimes, so I'd buy something for her any time we went anywhere together. I bought her all kinds of matching clothes.
I was surprised to learn they even made T-shirts that cost 50,000 yen apiece. But I was also impressed that there were people in Tokyo who could drop 50,000 yen on something like that.
And she bought those expensive T-shirts without a second thought. She said she wanted me to wear it, so I bought one for myself too.
I thought about how these flimsy T-shirts could cost 50,000... and my heart ached to know that an entire week's worth of pay would go to a single T-shirt.
The store clerk recommended some cool-looking clothes that matched the T-shirt, so I even bought some jeans and a sweater to go along with it.
Since my feet were in the door, I bought some matching boots too. Combined with the jeans, T-shirt, and sweater, it came to a total of nearly 300,000 yen.
When I pulled out my wallet to pay for all those clothes, I noticed it looked shabby too, so she and I went to a discount shop and bought a brand-name leather wallet.
And then I noticed how much my worn-out, cheap watch illustrated my meager upbringing.
I was prepared to dive in head first by that point, so I bought a brand-name watch from a pawn shop.
I felt two or three times as cool any time I was around her.
I felt like I was being reborn, and it felt good. I was no longer the pathetic Akira Otobe who was harassed by his friends and always fought with his parents.
I was a completely new Akira Otobe, full of youthful freedom, who took cute girls in cool clothes out in the constantly changing world of Tokyo.
That's what I wanted to be.
I ended up going to school less and less often.
I stopped talking to my classmates and spent more time with my coworkers.
And when I did occasionally go to class, I had no idea what any of it meant. And I no longer had friends to ask questions.
I didn't enter college to study in the first place.
...So, I finally stopped going to school at all.
However... living above my means was really hard.
No matter how much I worked, how much I spent on clothes, how much I treated myself to luxurious meals and kept up my appearances, somewhere deep down I still felt empty.
In order to fill that void, I kept spending time with my girlfriend, and I kept earning money at my part-time job.
In order to be the Akira Otobe I wanted to be, I needed money.
It didn't take long before I started taking out small loans to get the money for these activities.
My parents transferred money each month to pay for rent, but I started using that to pay the debts that were piling up left and right. I had accumulated debts ten times that amount.
...I even borrowed money to repay my debts.
"I believe in you, so I'll offer you a loan of 1 million yen with no collateral.@k@vS10/00/VT3A_kinYu1002. But let me just say, this is a one-time thing."

"I'm only doing this loan for you.@k@vS10/00/VT3A_kinYu1004. Because you're diligent and talented.@k@vS10/00/VT3A_kinYu1005. I'm lending it without collateral because I believe you'll be able to repay it.@k@vS10/00/VT3A_kinYu1006. This is just for you..."
Invited by those sweet words, I turned to street money and black-market lending... The compound interest continued to accumulate... and the outcome was inevitable.
I fled the apartment I was living in under the cover of night.
I lost contact with my girlfriend right around that time.
Eventually, collections agents started coming to my school and part-time job... so I had no choice but to find a new job.
I cycled through several jobs after that. I couldn't work in a single place for long, or the collections agents would find me.
I didn't have a place to call my own, so I couldn't make friends at work either. Nobody was interested in talking to a debt-ridden person like me in the first place.

But even if I worked morning until night and only spent the rest of my time sleeping, I couldn't raise more than 300,000 yen in a month. There was nothing I could do to keep up with the interest compounding each month, much less make payments toward the principal...
No matter how hard I worked, and even with a wage increase of 10 yen per hour, it did little to reduce my debt.
So I calculated how long it would take to repay my debt, living like this.
70 years.
I couldn't go back to college. I wasn't able to attend a single class, so I couldn't get any credit.
If I leave the university, I won't be able to find a job.
It's not impossible to get a proper job as a high school graduate... but it had already been so long since I graduated that I couldn't do it.
There's no way to explain that gap after entering college.
And even if I could get a real job, collections agents would show up there and ruin it soon enough.
I couldn't talk to my parents.

My parents would be shocked, outraged, and abusive. In the end, they'd forsake me. My father would beat me and my mother would coldly watch. They'd shout, "We believed in you!" with tears in their eyes.
It was plain to see what would happen back at home. ...I didn't want to go back to the countryside.
In other words, I had no choice but to live my life jumping between part-time jobs paying just 800 yen per hour.
And I'll spend an entire lifetime trying to repay a debt that can't be repaid in a lifetime.
...That crushing sense of vain effort caused me to give up on working altogether.

What awaited me afterwards was a life of peering out the shut curtains of my tiny four-and-a-half-tatami-sized apartment to make sure there weren't any collections agents between here and the convenience store 30 meters away.
I couldn't go shopping if collections agents were out there. I'd keep quiet and spend the rest of the day at home.
And traveling more than 30 meters away was out of the question. There was a risk I'd get kidnapped if the collections agents found me.
I even hesitated to step out of my apartment to take the trash out, in fear of collections agents wandering around.
...Then I finally realized.
My life had reached a deadlock.
I still had the same cell phone number, but I had no desire to check the incoming messages.
More than 20 messages arrived on the answering machine every day.

"You received life insurance when you borrowed this money, you know?@k@vS10/00/VT3A_kinYu1008. That way, you won't be a burden to your bereaved family.@k@vS10/00/VT3A_kinYu1009. ...Do you understand what I'm saying?"
*beep*
"Do you think you can keep running forever?"
*beep*
"For now, why not contact me?@k@vS10/00/VT3A_kinYu1012. I can introduce you to a good source of income."
"Just do what I tell you and I can have the interest on your debt forgiven.@k@vS10/00/VT3A_kinYu1014. Only the interest though."
*beep*
"Hello, Akiraaa...?"
That was my girlfriend's voice.

"It's been a long time~.@k@vS10/00/VT3A_Gfre1003. I went to your apartment for the first time in a while, but some scary people were wandering around so I couldn't get close.@k@vS10/00/VT3A_Gfre1004. What's going on?"
I didn't detect a hint of concern about me in her voice.
She probably found a new boyfriend after she got sick of me, but she got sick of him too and that's why she's calling me. ...I had a feeling that was the case.
Friends are disposable... I remember her saying that once before.
She goes out with people when they're convenient for her, and replaces them as soon as they become inconvenient.
According to her, we live in an ever-changing era so there's no way to know who might drop out of your life, or when.
You have to keep a watchful eye to ensure the people who were left behind don't hold you back.
In order to avoid falling victim to bad situations, it's important to always check the people you deal with to ensure they're worth keeping in touch with.
"Seems like you've had a lot of troubles, but do your best.@k@vS10/00/VT3A_Gfre1006. See ya."
*click* *beep* *beep* *beep*
That was all she said in her message.
There was no concern for me at all. No sign of her reaching out a helping hand.
Something bad happened, so it's best to keep her distance. That was the conclusion she reached.
In other words... she was cutting me off entirely.
I had already served my purpose.
As far as she was concerned, I was "dead weight."
Even though the two of us went to all those places together...
Even though we walked through all those places together, holding hands...
I clenched at one of those 50,000 yen T-shirts I only bought because it matched hers.
50,000 yen for this...?
This lifestyle was the price of that...?
And I was attracted to someone like her...?!
I held the T-shirt against my face and sobbed. I felt miserable and ashamed... but all I could do was cry.
Deep down, I realized that I had wasted my life on meaningless things.
As I continued sobbing, I pressed the play button on the answering machine once more.
"Akira, I have a friend willing to lend you some money. What do you think?@k@vS10/00/VT3A_freA1002. ...The interest is a bit steep, though."
*beep*
"This is KC sports club. Since your membership fee hasn't been paid for three months, we've had to cancel your membership.@k@vS10/00/VT3A_shainA1002. Regarding your unpaid balance..."
*beep*
"This is the Kaga University staff office. You still haven't filed your schedule for the upcoming semester.@k@vS10/00/VT3A_jimuA1002. Please come to the university at your earliest convenience..."
*beep*

"This is the store manager of the "Million Stones" tavern. Don't come back again.@k@vS10/00/VT3A_Tkin1002. On account of your chronic unannounced absences, you're fired.@k@vS10/00/VT3A_Tkin1003. You really need to get your shit together."
*beep*
None of it mattered...
These were people I thought I was close to, people I respected, people I believed would offer to help if I was in trouble, but every one of them turned their noses up at me.
The reality of that was difficult to bear alone.
But... it couldn't be helped. I brought this on myself.
This is the price of the selfish life I've been living...
Nobody will lend a helping hand to someone like me.
Everyone will look down on me for putting up a fake front. I've pushed everyone away, so I don't have any friends left.
And then, the last message on the answering machine was...
"Akira... are you doing well?"
It was a familiar voice.
"I can't get ahold of you on the phone, and all the letters I send you get sent back...@k@vS10/00/VT3A_otobeM1012. Are you doing all right?"
My chest tightened.
What...? After all this time...?
I kept gazing at the flashing button on the answering machine.
I should've been disgusted to hear my Mom's voice... but as soon as I did, tears came overflowing.
Even though I despised so many things about my Mom...
What is this...? Why am I crying...?
Mom always tried to do whatever Dad wanted... and she was always good at reading his mood.
So, Mom always tried to control me to meet Dad's whim.
Say this to improve Dad's mood, do that to make Dad happy. But don't do anything he wouldn't like.
My father said that men had no use for literature, so he only ever bought me scientific journals.
Even though I liked geography, my father believed that history was more important, so he forced me to learn Japanese history.
He said that music wasn't suitable for men, so I had to abandon the woodwind concert ensemble I had wanted to enter since elementary school.
In the end, I joined the kendo club... but it didn't suit me at all. I just wasn't very good, so my upperclassmen bullied me over my lack of drive to improve.
I lost all my self-confidence, so I started acting more timid and distancing myself from my classmates. The boys made fun of me all the time, and the girls constantly giggled at me behind my back.
Yeah... I still remember those miserable days in the countryside.
I don't want to go back there, ever again...
But... I... can't stay here either...
I... have nowhere to go...

"...Your father told me not to call, but I was just feeling a bit concerned.@k@vS10/00/VT3A_otobeM1014. So, how is Tokyo?@k@vS10/00/VT3A_otobeM1015. Working hard on your studies?@k@vS10/00/VT3A_otobeM1016. It would be nice to hear your voice once in a while."
As if... I could call them.
No matter why she called... I decided it was best not to tell her about how I was feeling...

"I may not understand your dreams, but...@k@vS10/00/VT3A_otobeM1018. please do your best.@k@vS10/00/VT3A_otobeM1019. You can do it if you give it your all.@k@vS10/00/VT3A_otobeM1020. I'll always be there for you."
That can't be true...
Even though I thought that, I still wanted to believe in my mother's love.
Mom will always be there for me.
Is that really true...? Was she... actually trying to encourage me...?
...Then please help me, Mom.
I don't know what to do... and it's painful...
Please help...
"Your father is still a bit upset with you,@k@vS10/00/VT3A_otobeM1022. but I'm sure he'd accept you if he could see you living a respectable life.@k@vS10/00/VT3A_otobeM1023. Do your best, Akira."
*click* *beep* *beep* *beep*
"Message complete. To listen again, press 1. To save..."
I erased all the messages and stared at the phone.
My vision was fogged over with tears.
If he could see me living a respectable life...?
It was no longer possible for me to live a proper life.
Where did I go wrong...?
What was it I wanted...?
I felt so miserable I truly wanted to die.
"Uu... uuu...!@k@vS10/41/410200659_1. Uwaaaaaaahh, uwaaaaaaaahh...!!"
...I thought I was special.
I didn't have a dream or a concrete something to work towards, but I swore back then I would try my best.
But I never found an opportunity like that.
When I first came to Tokyo, I felt like I was reborn as a different person... I was completely ecstatic.
Somewhere deep in my heart, I realized I was living a depraved life. But I thought I could pay back the money I borrowed. If I could find something to be passionate about...
But... I wasn't someone special...
I couldn't find anything to be passionate about...
"Do your best, Akira."
Mom's words echoed inside my ears.
Mom...
I can't go back now.
And I can't go forward either.
Thinking that, and feeling completely helpless... I stepped outside for the first time in ages and felt the raindrops strike me...
And when I came to my senses... I was on the internet... and had reached a specific site.

177 Name:Akkun:2006/05/05(Friday) 23:55:29.71@rID:Uqv1UM21@r@rDid you see this issue of the Weekly Seven?@rIt mentions an occult spot called Hinamizawa village.@r@rI don't think they'd find us in a place like that.@rWhat do you think?

257 Name:Machi:2006/05/07(Sunday) 01:15:13.27@rID:Lv/AL2xg@r@rI've read the Weekly Seven. Sounds good.@rIt mentioned the gas disaster happened in June,@rso there should be even fewer people around then♪.@r@rHow about the second Monday in June?@rIf you can, I'm sure the weather will be nice.
416 Name:Chiaki:2006/05/13(Friday) 03:14:33@rID:khBZSuoI@r@rI hope it'll go well this time!@r@r@r@r@rIs it ok to do this with just three people?
Somewhere out there... I felt something resembling human warmth... and when I noticed that, I wrote too.
794 Name:Otobe:2006/05/27(Friday) 00:00:07@rID:kt8TwvtL@r@rI'd like to come, too.@rLooking forward to it.
I let out a huge sigh after writing that.
Death would be more appropriate for someone like me.
I no longer cared... about being alive...
"And so, I gathered together... with three like-minded people.@k@vS10/41/410200661. The four of us decided we wanted to die together in Hinamizawa..."
One of the lights in the assembly hall started to flicker erratically.
Nevertheless, Otobe completely ignored the lightbulb and continued his story.
"Still, this is awful...@k@vS10/62/410101371. I never thought my article would be used to determine a suicide spot.@k@vS10/62/410101372. ...It's too much."
"I'm sorry..."
"...Ah, wait, does that mean...@k@vS10/62/410101374. that you were also... in that car with the three people?"
"...Yeah."
Otobe continued drooping apologetically as he nodded to me.
"...This might be a strange thing to say, but even though I came here to die...@k@vS10/41/410200665. I... was having fun."

"Everyone... was so nice.@k@vS10/41/410200667. Today... well, the date has already changed, so yesterday...@k@vS10/41/410200668. Anyway, even though I only met these people for the first time yesterday, they felt like they had been my friends for a long time."
Otobe began speaking once more.@k And nobody tried to interrupt him.
The sky was nice and blue that day, just like Machi-chan wanted.
We all lived fairly far apart, so in the end we decided to meet at Tokyo Station.
The first time I came to Tokyo Station, back in last spring, was when I felt my first taste of the city.

Businessmen dressed in fancy suits and gorgeous office ladies that looked like they belonged in magazines were hanging out at exotic foreign coffee shops, and the station was surrounded by tall, stylish buildings...
The clamor of people was like the omnipresent background noise of an urban area.
But... now I was able to recognize that every last one of them had no bearing on my life.
Back then, everything was shining bright... but now, it all looked so dingy.
All the things I once longed for were ostentatious phonies.
"Nice to meet you, I'm the user called Akkun. My name is Akutagawa."
Akkun, huh...?
The man smiling and bowing his head in front of the car was, surprisingly, a man roughly in his 40s.
He had a gentlemanly aura, like all the businessmen from around here.
A man like him wants to commit suicide...?
I couldn't hide my surprise.
"And I'm Machi, short for Machiko.@k@vS10/00/VT3A_machi1002. We won't be knowing each other very long, but feel free to call me Machi."
Machi-chan looked like she was still in her teens. She was wearing really pretty makeup, but she was probably still in high school.
Her skin was shiny, and she had a miniskirt stretched across her slender legs. She seemed like a perfectly normal girl, the likes of which you'd find anywhere.
Why would a girl like her...
"I'm Chiaki.@k@vS10/00/VT3A_chia1002. Chiaki is actually my real name, and my online handle too."
Chiaki-san bashfully said that.
She was a beautiful woman in her late 20s. She had a gentle demeanor, and gave off the atmosphere of someone living a wealthy lifestyle.
She was dressed in nicely tailored clothes like me, but her elegance and dignity were out of this world.
She felt like a platinum level, truly refined lady.
"Nice to meet you... I'm Otobe...@k@vS10/41/410200670. Otobe is my real name."
As I bowed my head, I looked around to make sure no collections agents were nearby.
"You look nervous, but don't worry, Otobe-kun.@k@vS10/00/VT3A_akuta1003. After all... this is everyone's first time dying."
Akutagawa-san could tell I was feeling nervous, so he gave me a reassuring smile as he said that.
He thinks I'm afraid to die.
But the truth is... I'm not scared at all.
The fact that I was going to die soon actually had me feeling excited.
I was feeling more stressed about my first real-life meeting with my internet friends.
I was about to get in a car with strangers and go somewhere. But... I was happy to be going with other people, rather than on my own.
Eventually, the car took off with Akutagawa-san behind the wheel. I took the passenger seat and the ladies were in the back seats.
Even though I was in the passenger seat, I ducked my head down to avoid being spotted by any debt collectors who might be looking at the car.
I was able to raise my head and feel some peace of mind after the car got up to a decent speed.
Chiaki-san held some sweets out to me.
"Would you like one?@k@vS10/00/VT3A_chia1004. They're from my local bakery, and they're quite delicious."
"Thanks..."
The candy was full of butter and brandy, very tasty.
The flavor itself was great, of course... but it was a nice feeling too.
Sharing something tasty with someone else.
That alone was enough to remind me what it was like to experience happiness.
"I brought something too... Care for a chocolate?"
I received a chocolate from Machi-chan.
It must've been foreign chocolate, because the package was in English. It looked nice and sticky on the inside, too. That was the first time I ever tasted chocolate like that.

"Ahaha. It's so sweet, huh?@k@vS10/00/VT3A_machi1005. But there's no way I can eat them all!@k@vS10/00/VT3A_machi1006. I bought a whole box because it was on sale,@k@vS10/00/VT3A_machi1007. but it ended up being way too much.@k@vS10/00/VT3A_machi1008. Care for another?"
Machi-chan said that gleefully and handed me another. Her smile was bright and carefree.
I never thought someone could smile that earnestly about eating junk food.
"This is some great weather, huh?@k@vS10/00/VT3A_akuta1005. Nice and sunny.@k@vS10/00/VT3A_akuta1006. ...This is our last day, so I hope the weather stays like this."
Once he said that... I remembered we were on our way to die...
It didn't feel real.
I fiddled with my phone.
No incoming calls today.
......"Do your best, Akira."
Mom's voice echoed inside my ears again.
I hadn't received a call from my parents since then. And of course, I didn't have the courage to call them back.
Do your best, Akira.
If she had said, "Please come home," instead of, "Do your best," back then...
"Are you that concerned about your phone?"
After playing with my phone in the passenger seat all this time, Akutagawa-san called out to me.
"Ah, no..."
"If there's someone out there who you'd like to hear one last time... why not give them a call?"
"Ah, that's ok.@k@vS10/41/410200674. ...I don't have anyone like that..."
"Really?@k@vS10/00/VT3A_chia1006. ...You still have time. There's no need to worry."
"I'm fine.@k@vS10/41/410200676. Really... that's all."
I powered off my cell phone.
Eventually, the car pulled into Shishibone city and began climbing the mountain road.
"It's almost time..."
Machi-chan reapplied her mascara and touched up her lipstick.
Her face looked radiant, like she was all set for a date with her lover.
After arriving in Hinamizawa, we parked the car in a suitable location and started making preparations.
Even up until this point, it felt like a fun group project.
"No ghosts, huh?@k@vS10/00/VT3A_machi1012. They called this the village of the dead, so I expected something more.@k@vS10/00/VT3A_machi1013. I was hoping to see my dead family."
"But... anyway, I'll be able to see them soon enough."
"...Your family is dead?"
"Yeah..."
After that one word from Machi-chan, everyone began sharing their personal stories.
I was surprised to hear it.
Everyone had their own tragic episodes that drove them to suicide.
Machi-chan lost her entire family, Akutagawa-san was saddled with the debt of others, Chiaki-san lost her husband... All of them did everything in their power to keep going before turning to suicide.
I, on the other hand...
I was full of myself, and I did whatever I liked... I just fooled around for my own satisfaction, and suffered the consequences of my actions...
I was ashamed. ...Honestly, I was so ashamed.
"...Even up until today, I couldn't stop thinking about how I could have done more for my husband."

"I wanted to tell him, by any means necessary... I wanted to apologize for not being by his side at the most precious of times.@k@vS10/00/VT3A_chia1009. It doesn't matter how much money you earn. It's pointless if you never have a chance to spend it."
Chiaki-san reached into her handbag as she said that.
"This is what he gained from all his tireless work.@k@vS10/00/VT3A_chia1011. I don't need it anymore.@k@vS10/00/VT3A_chia1012. So, I thought I should deliver it to him myself."
She pulled a bank book, a signature stamp, and a cash card out of her bag.
My eyes fixated on those items.
I heard a faint voice... inside my head.
This is what he gained from all his tireless work...?
Just how much is in there...?
With that money, could I start all over??
"He worked his body ragged... and in the end, he worked himself to death."
"...I wish I had told him not to work himself so hard... but I pretended not to see it."
"Even though he earned all that money to give me a carefree life, I resented him for not spending enough time with me.@k@vS10/00/VT3A_chia1016. ...I wasn't fit to be his wife."
"From here on... couldn't you marry someone else and start over?"
Even though I knew I was speaking out of place, I couldn't help but say something.
That's not something worth dying over, is it...?
Because... that was enough money to keep on living, without bothering anyone.
I don't have anything... and there's nothing else I can do, so I chose to die.
And yet... why?
"Otobe-kun, that's none of your..."
"No, it's all right.@k@vS10/00/VT3A_chia1019. He was the only one I loved."

"From now on, I'll never love anyone else...@k@vS10/00/VT3A_chia1021. He was my only reason to live.@k@vS10/00/VT3A_chia1022. Next time, I hope we can hold hands as husband and wife, and stay together forever..."
Chiaki-san interrupted Akutagawa-san to say all that.
Chiaki-san was about to die with all that wealth.
My heart was beating like mad while I listened to her story.
...To start over.
With that money, I could pay off my debts and start a new life...!
As I listened to Chiaki-san's story on the road to death, a feeling of hope started to emerge inside me.
"Well then, would you like to share your story next, Otobe-kun...?"
"Oh... Sure."
Before I realized it... it was my turn to share my story.
But... now my heart was bursting with hope.
I no longer had a reason for wanting to die.
...I wasn't sure exactly how much she had, but with all the wealth Chiaki-san had right there... With that money, I could start over...!
Yes. I don't want to die.
I don't want to die, I don't want to die, I don't want to die, I don't want to die, I don't want to die, I don't want to die.
...Not when I still have a chance.
My selfish desire to live.
My pathetic, ridiculous desire to see another day.
Haunted by those thoughts, I struggled to force a smile and painfully bit down on my tongue.

"...I won't force you to say it.@k@vS10/00/VT3A_chia1024. Let's stop this for now.@k@vS10/00/VT3A_chia1025. ...It's ok, Otobe-kun.@k@vS10/00/VT3A_chia1026. It will all be over soon.@k@vS10/00/VT3A_chia1027. We'll finally be released from our suffering."
"...Yeah."
That was all I could say.
I still didn't want to die...
I wasn't so attached to living until just now.
"Alright... everyone...@k@vS10/00/VT3A_akuta1011. Let's meet again on the other side."
"Goodbye...@k@vS10/00/VT3A_chia1029. Akutagawa-san, Otobe-kun, Machi-chan...@k@vS10/00/VT3A_chia1030. Our time together was short, but thank you."
"Goodbye..."
That's right, I can start over. I can start everything over.
I can still do it.
The sunset stained the sky a deep red.
I could hear the cry of the higurashi coming through the door.
Machi-chan said she was glad it was sunny.
Chiaki-san said this was the perfect place to die.
Akutagawa-san said we'll meet on the other side.
"...Hehe.@k@vS10/41/410200684. Ehehehehehehehehe, ahahahahahahahahahaha.@k@vS10/41/410200685. Aahahahahahahahahahahahahah!!"
Seriously.
Sure it's nice weather, but what's so great about that?
This is a vacant area without any people nearby. That's probably the worst place to die.
And meet on the other side? ...It's still too soon for me!!
And... after everyone fell asleep, I climbed out of the car alone.
Outside, the sunset shined down on the thick gray clouds in the sky. And in no time, the surrounding area was covered in darkness, impossible to tell right from left.
But all I could think about was tightly holding the property I took from Chiaki-san... as I ran down the pitch-black mountain road...
"But it started to rain as soon as I left the car. The road was muddy and I stumbled over and over...@k@vS10/41/410200687. Then, as I ran around in total darkness, I lost my way..."
I glanced at my watch. It was 2 AM.
Five men and women, gathered together in this assembly hall with a broken light bulb.
One of them surrounded by the remaining four, as if to condemn him for his stupid mistakes...
"So... you kept running, and you stumbled on a tree root or something, tumbled down a hill, and that was how you ended up meeting me."
"...That's right."
"So... you made up that story about the car with your friends disappearing?"
"Yes...@k@vS10/41/410200690. I'm sorry... I'm really sorry..."
"So, you knew all of the people who committed suicide?"
"Right...@k@vS10/41/410200692. I'm sorry... I'm sorry..."
"What the hell...?"
"You amassed debt, decided to take your life with barely a second thought...@k@vS10/43/410400494. And what's more, you took your friend's bank book... and ran."
Sonozaki-san stood up. Then, she donned a more harsh expression than she had worn until now... and spoke up.
"How utterly spineless...@k@vS10/43/410400496. You're disgusting."
Otobe fell down on his face on the spot, with his shoulders trembling. In that moment, all the tears strung across his face spilled down to the floor.
"I'm sorry... I'm really sorry..."
"...Wait.@k@vS10/62/410101380. Does that mean... you're also... the one who kidnapped Kurosawa...?"
I, too, looked down at Otobe with disgust. ...But as soon as he realized the meaning of my words, he fiercely looked up with his face still littered in tears.
"I-I don't know anything about that...!@k@vS10/41/410200697. I know nothing about Kurosawa-san...!"
"You idiot, do you expect us to believe that?!@k@vS10/62/410101382. You robbed the dead and you've been lying to us this whole time. How can we believe anything you have to say?"
"But... I really don't know anything!@k@vS10/41/410200699. Mion-san, please believe me!@k@vS10/41/410200700. Honestly... I don't know anything!"
"Yeah, I already know that much.@k@vS10/43/410400498. ...There's no way a weakling like you could successfully catch a living person, kill them, and hide the evidence."
"...Indeed."
Miyuki-chan clearly agreed with Sonozaki-san.
Why...? How can the two of them be so sure of that?
What makes them so confident...?
Miyuki-chan was gazing straight at Towada-san.
Her expression made it look like she was silently questioning her.
